extra.reasoning: The monotonicity property concerns flipping `syncope` from false to true while holding the other four inputs fixed, and requires acuity to be no lower afterward. Crucially, `syncope` does not appear in any rule (r1 reads vision_loss, r2 vomiting, r3 hemoptysis, r4 abdominal_pain) and is not used in the default. Therefore acuity is fully determined by the other four inputs, independent of syncope. For every one of the 16 combinations of the other inputs, acuity(syncope=false) equals acuity(syncope=true). Equality satisfies "no lower than before" (the property requires non-decrease, not strict increase). Since flipping syncope never changes the output at all, it can never lower acuity. No counterexample exists; the property holds for the entire input space.
